quote.text: In every walk with nature, one receives far more than he seeks.
quote.author: John Muir

fun_fact.text: The Strait of Juan de Fuca, which you can see from Port Angeles, is named after a Greek sailor who sailed for Spain in 1592. He was actually born Ioannis Phokas, but adopted the Spanish version of his name. The strait is one of the few inland waterways in the world where you can spot both orcas and gray whales!

short_read.title: How Plants Know When to Bloom
short_read.teaser: Scientists have discovered that plants can actually count the days until spring using special proteins that act like tiny calendars. This fascinating process explains why tulips and daffodils know exactly when to peek through the soil!

medium_read.title: The Secret Language of Hummingbirds
medium_read.teaser: These tiny jewels of the bird world communicate through an elaborate system of chirps, buzzes, and even wing beats that vary by species. Learn about their remarkable migration journeys and how they remember every flower they've visited.

long_read.title: The Gardens of Hungary: A Journey Through Time
long_read.teaser: From medieval monastery gardens to the grand estates of the Austro-Hungarian Empire, Hungary's horticultural heritage is rich and beautiful. This article explores how Hungarian gardeners preserved rare plant varieties and created some of Europe's most stunning landscapes.

port_angeles.title: Black-Tailed Deer on the Peninsula
port_angeles.description: June is a magical time to spot Columbian black-tailed deer fawns around Port Angeles and Olympic National Park. These adorable spotted babies are born in late spring and stay hidden in tall grass while their mothers forage nearby. If you see a fawn alone, don't worry—mom is usually just out getting food and will return soon!

puzzle_1.question: A hummingbird's heart beats approximately ___ times per minute while flying.
puzzle_1.correct_response: Exactly right! That tiny heart is beating faster than we can even imagine!
puzzle_1.close_response: You're very close! It's actually about 1,200 beats per minute—incredibly fast!
puzzle_1.wrong_response: Those tiny hearts beat an amazing 1,200 times per minute! That's about 20 beats every second.
puzzle_1.learn_more_text: A hummingbird's heart rate can slow down to about 250 beats per minute when they're resting at night, and they enter a hibernation-like state called torpor to conserve energy.

puzzle_2.question: Which Hungarian word means 'butterfly'?
puzzle_2.correct_response: Wonderful! Pillangó is such a beautiful word for such a beautiful creature!
puzzle_2.wrong_response: The word is 'pillangó'—doesn't it sound lovely? (Madár means bird, virág means flower, and méhecske means little bee.)
puzzle_2.learn_more_text: The word 'pillangó' may come from the Hungarian word 'pillanni' which means 'to glance' or 'to flutter,' perfectly describing how butterflies move through the garden!

puzzle_3.question: What type of whale is most commonly seen in the Strait of Juan de Fuca during summer months?
puzzle_3.correct_response: Yes! Orcas are the stars of the Strait in summer, following the salmon runs. You might even see them from the Port Angeles waterfront!
puzzle_3.wrong_response: Actually, it's the orca (killer whale)! These magnificent black and white whales come to the Strait to feast on salmon, and the Southern Resident orcas are a beloved symbol of the Pacific Northwest.
puzzle_3.learn_more_text: Three pods of Southern Resident orcas—J, K, and L pods—regularly travel through the Strait of Juan de Fuca. Each whale has unique markings and is known by name to researchers who have studied them for decades.
puzzle_3.learn_more_label: Learn more — Center for Whale Research

game.title: Bubble Shooter
game.description: A relaxing bubble puzzle game — aim and pop matching colored bubbles. Easy to pick up, fun to play!
